slides.title: The AI/ML Cooling Challenge
slides.subtitle: Rack densities are pushing past what air cooling can handle.

bullets.text: now common in AI/ML clusters — well past the practical limit of air cooling.
moves heat at the chip via cold plates + a dedicated secondary loop. Lower power, smaller floor footprint.
a standardized, openly-published, high-performance CDU at multi-megawatt scale — the bridge between facility chilled water and rack cold plates.

bullets.bold_lead: Project Deschutes targets exactly that gap:
bullets.text: 2 MW thermal capacity, industry-leading hydraulic performance, open and available to every supplier.

source_refs.source: Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0

slides.title: Where the CDU Lives: FWS → CDU → TCS
slides.subtitle: The CDU is the precise control point between the building water plant and rack cold plates.

figure.alt: Silent animation of cold supply and warm return flow between the Facility Water System, Project Deschutes CDU, and Technology Cooling System. The two fluid loops exchange heat without mixing.
figure.caption: The CDU pulls only as much facility water as needed to hold a fixed 3°C approach temperature, while delivering 500 GPM at 80–90 psi to the IT loop.

source_refs.source: Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0

slides.title: Built on 7+ Years at Hyperscale
slides.subtitle: First announced at the OCP EMEA Summit 2025, iterated to v1.0 through open community feedback.

bullets.text: Informed by more than 7 years of liquid cooling at hyperscale.
Ground-truth data on what fails, how, and what to redesign.

bullets.bold_lead: First announced at OCP EMEA Summit 2025.
bullets.text: Google released the v0.8 draft for public community feedback.
Months of supplier and operator input, design-for-manufacturing refinements, and updated pump reliability data.
BOM, manufacturing quality, packaging, barcode labeling, validation, and FRU procedures all published.

figure.alt: Project Deschutes CDU overview — two views showing the unit with labeled callouts.
figure.caption: Figure 3.3.1 from the v1.0 spec — CDU overview with primary/secondary connections, control enclosure, pumps, and heat exchangers labeled.

source_refs.source: Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0

slides.title: Headline Specifications (Spec Table 3.1)
rows: 2 pumps N+1 · 3 HX in parallel · dual-feed power

source_refs.source: Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0

slides.title: What You Learned
items: First open CDU specification contributed to the Open Compute Project — Google's 5th-generation design.
Engineered for AI/ML: 2 MW thermal capacity at a 3 °C approach temperature, 80–90 psi available on the IT loop.
Sits as the precise control point between the Facility Water System and the Technology Cooling System.
All numbers traceable to the official v1.0 spec, dated February 26, 2026.
First announced at OCP EMEA Summit 2025, then refined through open community feedback to ship as v1.0.

source_refs.source: Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0

slides.title: Check Your Deschutes Mental Model
slides.subtitle: Answer once to continue. Correctness is not required, and you can retry.

questions.prompt: What is the Deschutes CDU's role in the FWS → CDU → TCS architecture?

choices.text: It bridges facility water and the clean IT coolant loop, controlling coolant delivery while transferring heat back to the facility side.
It replaces the building chilled-water plant entirely.
It is only a monitoring device for rack airflow.
It distributes rack electrical feeds in the hot aisle.

questions.feedback_correct: Correct. Deschutes is the controlled liquid-to-liquid bridge between the Facility Water System and Technology Cooling System.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Review the architecture slide. The CDU sits between FWS and TCS; it does not replace the facility plant or act as an electrical distribution device.

questions.prompt: Which headline values belong to the Project Deschutes v1.0 CDU specification?

choices.text: 2 MW thermal load at a 3 °C approach temperature.
500 GPM IT flow with 80–90 psi available differential pressure.
500 GPM facility flow at 15–30 psi at rated load.
A closed, proprietary design available only to one supplier.

questions.feedback_correct: Correct. Those values summarize the thermal, IT-side hydraulic, and facility-side targets emphasized in Module 1.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Look back at Spec Table 3.1. Deschutes is open, and the key numbers include 2 MW, 3 °C approach, 500 GPM, and the published pressure ranges.

slides.title: CDU Architecture & Performance
slides.subtitle: Opening the unit — the v1.0 internals, end to end

bullets.text: 65.25” W × 91.44” H × 42.29” D; 100” max packaged height.
5,300 lb dry, 6,900 lb wet — plan floor structure and seismic anchoring accordingly.
Rolled into place, anchored, plumbed.
Wetted materials limited to stainless, copper, EPDM. No nickel-plated brass on the secondary loop.
IEC 60068-2-27 operating (3.5 G, 11 ms half-sine); 30 G non-op; ASTM D4169 transportation.

figure.alt: Two CAD views of the Project Deschutes CDU showing primary and secondary side connections, control enclosure, pumps, HX, seismic interface.
figure.caption: Figure 3.3.1 — the Project Deschutes CDU. The unit is engineered to survive shipping, rolling, and seismic events, not just operate cleanly.

source_refs.source: Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0
Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0

slides.title: Mechanical Features: Built for Long-Term Operation

bullets.text: Optimized to minimize integration risk, reduce pressure drop, simplify service touchpoints.
500 µm primary strainer; 25–44 µm main secondary; 0.2 µm side-stream microfiltration at ~3 GPM.
purges dissolved gases continuously.
v1.0 added front-side serviceability — direct lesson from prior generations.
Preferred for long-term elastomer stability.

figure.alt: Annotated photos showing how Victaulic and Tri-clamp fittings are arranged around the heat exchangers and the manifold.
figure.caption: Figure 3.3.4 — optimized mix of Victaulic and Tri-clamp fittings minimizes integration risk and pressure drop.

source_refs.source: Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0
Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0

slides.title: 500 GPM at 80–90 psi — With Published Curves

bullets.text: 500 GPM @ 80–90 psi available dP — enough head for rack manifolds + dozens of cold plates.
two pumps in N+1 (spec p. 19 curve, PG25 fluid).
one pump, reduced pressure (spec p. 20) — enough time to schedule a swap rather than declare an outage.
500 GPM @ 15–30 psi at rated load — only as much as the unit actually needs.
0–130 psig operating, 130 psi pressure relief, Parker N-S-P-251 ORB6 quick-disconnect telemetry ports.

figure.alt: CDU secondary-side hydraulic curve, two-pump (N+1) operation, PG25 fluid.
figure.caption: Figure 5.1.1 — secondary-side hydraulic performance (available pressure) with 2-pump operation.

source_refs.source: Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0
Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0

slides.title: 3 °C Approach at 2 MW — Better Across Most Loads

bullets.text: Lower pressure drop, redundancy for service.
Alfa Laval CB210-276AH (Item 30751 6274 3).
3 °C approach @ 2 MW heat load, 500 GPM each side.

bullets.bold_lead: Real performance is better than headline.
bullets.text: Table 5.3 publishes 7 operating points — e.g. 2.3 °C @ 2,150 kW / 545 GPM; 1.6 °C @ 640 kW / 162 GPM.
18–55 °C; DI water (ChemTreat, Garratt-Callahan, Veolia formulations) or PG25.

figure.alt: Heat exchanger thermal performance curve — approach temperature drops with flow.
figure.caption: Figure 5.3.2 — approach temperature vs flow at 2 MW heat load, dual-pass heat exchanger.

source_refs.source: Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0
Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0
Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0

bullets.text: Speck TOEMA065200 — 500 GPM @ 112 psi, 37 kW, seal-less. N+1 redundant.
ABB ACH580-31-065A-4 (NEMA Type 12) — v1.0 added serviceability accessories.
Belimo B6500.
Alfa Laval CB210-276AH × 3 in parallel.
T + P on all 4 manifolds; flow ± 0.5%; per-pump RPM & power; 3-zone leak detection; coolant pH + electrical conductivity.

figure.alt: Annotated photo of the secondary (IT) side of the CDU with pumps, filters, heat exchangers, and the flow meter labeled.
figure.caption: Figure 3.3.2 — secondary (technical/IT) flow loop features: 4× filters, secondary flow meter, pumps, isolation valves, Victaulic fittings.

source_refs.source: Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0
Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0
Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0

slides.title: PLC with Two Control Loops + Dual-Feed Power
slides.subtitle: Modbus/TCP, IPv4 + IPv6 dual stack, hot-swappable redundant subcomponents.

figure.alt: Silent animation showing the PLC using differential-pressure feedback to control secondary pump speed, approach-temperature feedback to control the primary valve, and redundant AC feeds maintaining control availability.
figure.caption: Two control loops run concurrently: pump speed holds the secondary differential-pressure setpoint while primary-valve position holds the 3 °C approach temperature.

source_refs.source: Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0
Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0
Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0

slides.title: What You Learned
items: Physical: 65 × 91 × 42 in, ~6,900 lb wet. Built for shipping, rolling, anchoring.
Filtration is layered: 500 µm → 25–44 µm → 0.2 µm side-stream — designed for years of clean operation.
Hydraulic: 500 GPM @ 80–90 psi, published 2-pump and 1-pump curves.
Thermal: 3 °C approach @ 2 MW — better than spec across most of the operating envelope.
Components named: Speck pumps, ABB VFDs, Belimo valve, Alfa Laval HX.
PLC: Modbus/TCP, IPv4 + IPv6, two default loops (pump speed, primary valve), hot-swap subcomponents.

slides.title: Check Your Architecture Understanding
slides.subtitle: Answer once to continue. Correctness is not required, and you can retry.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes the published secondary-side hydraulic target?

choices.text: 500 GPM on the IT side with 80–90 psi of available differential pressure.
500 GPM on the facility side with 80–90 psi of available differential pressure.
15–30 psi available on the IT side at rated load.
A single fixed-flow point with no published performance curves.

questions.feedback_correct: Correct. The IT or secondary side target is 500 GPM with 80–90 psi available downstream.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Review the hydraulic performance slide. The 80–90 psi available differential pressure is on the IT side; facility pressure is a lower range.

questions.prompt: Which controls and telemetry statements are supported by Module 2?

choices.text: The PLC supports Modbus/TCP with IPv4 and IPv6 dual-stack networking.
Pump speed is controlled to maintain IT-side differential pressure.
The primary control valve modulates to maintain approach temperature.
The secondary flow meter is designed for powered hot-swap service.

questions.feedback_correct: Correct. The control model combines PLC networking, pump speed control, and primary-valve approach-temperature control.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Check the controls and maintenance details. The secondary flow meter requires CDU power-off service, while the PLC and control loops stay central to operation.

slides.title: Deployment, Supply Chain & Call to Action
slides.subtitle: From spec to data-center floor

bullets.text: Companion data-center structure to deploy the CDU into.
28” W, up to 50” D, 80.7–90.55” H — shoulder-to-shoulder, mix-and-match depths.
54” cold; 68.4” hot (with RDHx) or 102” hot (without).
Fits most colo data centers worldwide — drop-in, not facility-rebuild.
100.5” (wall manifold), 115.5”, 127.5”, 139.5” (cable trays). Above that = fungible PCW pipe space.

figure.alt: Annotated diagram of the Redmond Hot Aisle Containment structure with X-beam supports, cable tray hangers, base plates, and busduct mounting.
figure.caption: Figure 18.5.1 — Redmond HAC structure. Vertical posts on 5” × 5” base plates support lateral X-beams for wall-manifold mounts; cable tray hangers attach to the verticals.

source_refs.source: Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0
Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0
Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0

bullets.bold_lead: 8 × 600 A bus ducts in the hot aisle
bullets.text: (4 per side) feed power down the row.
Each rack fed by 4 feeds, only 3 needed at full load — single feed loss is invisible to the rack.
supported at 1-second timescales with 3 of 4 feeds operational.
5-pin, 30/32 A, 230/240 VAC Wye — global standard, EMEA / NA / Japan.
UPS redundancy ≥ feed redundancy; generator redundancy must support all 4 feeds.

figure.alt: Floor layout showing CDU placement relative to the Redmond structure with primary and secondary piping connections.
figure.caption: Figure 18.13.1 — CDU placement on the floor with primary (facility) and secondary (IT) piping connections.

slides.title: Three-Tier Supplier Model
slides.subtitle: Designed for 10–12 week worldwide lead times at competitive cost.
rows: Approved Vendor List — active production for Google deployments
Reserved Approved — fully qualified standby, ready to activate
Recommended Vendor List — self-qualified to spec; can sell to other CSPs and colos
Per critical component (HX, pump, VFD, PSU, piping, sensors)

source_refs.source: Project Deschutes: Google's Open Spec CDU contribution to the industry

bullets.text: Both filters, side-stream microfiltration, expansion tank, control panel — all front-access.
Pump A front, Pump B rear (or side if blocked). Controlled break points + isolation valves + drain ports.
VFD clearance accessories + front-serviceable expansion tank — direct lessons from prior generations.
Secondary flow meter requires CDU power-off — scheduled-event service.
Section 17: Manufacturer ID, GPN, MPN, S/N, COO, date code on a PMS 803C yellow label.

figure.alt: Pump A serviced from the front of the CDU with controlled break points at the inlet and outlet.
figure.caption: Figure 13.2.1 — Pump A service from the front of the CDU. Both pumps have isolation valves, drain ports, and connectorized power.

source_refs.source: Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0
Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0
Project Deschutes: Data Center Facilities V1.0.0

slides.title: Isolate, Drain, De-energize, Disconnect, Remove
slides.subtitle: The redundant pump maintains flow while the service branch is made safe for removal.

figure.alt: Silent animation of a contained pump service sequence: the redundant pump maintains flow while the service branch is isolated, drained, de-energized, disconnected, and removed through the front service envelope.
figure.caption: The service sequence protects people, coolant containment, and continuous operation. Pump A exits from the front; Pump B normally exits from the rear.

slides.title: What You Learned
items: Redmond HAC ships with the same spec — together they form a complete 2 MW liquid-cooled bay sized for global colos.
Power & cooling co-designed: 4-makes-3 redundancy, 8 × 600 A bus ducts, 90 kW per rack-spot.
Front-serviceable by design — filters, pumps, expansion tank, control panel; v1.0 added VFD + expansion-tank serviceability.
Three-tier supplier model: AVL (2) + R-AVL (2) + RVL (6+), targeting 10–12 week worldwide lead times.
The spec is open, evolving, and inviting community input — future revisions will incorporate manufacturing and validation feedback.

slides.title: Check Deployment Decisions
slides.subtitle: Answer once to continue. Correctness is not required, and you can retry.

questions.prompt: What does the Redmond interface contribute alongside the Deschutes CDU?

choices.text: A companion hot-aisle containment and deployment structure for the liquid-cooled bay.
A replacement for all rack-level electrical redundancy.
A proprietary software-only control plane with no physical layout guidance.
A rule that every deployment must rebuild the facility shell from scratch.

questions.feedback_correct: Correct. Redmond gives the CDU a defined, repeatable physical deployment environment.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Review the Redmond slide. Its role is a companion hot-aisle containment and bay interface, not a replacement for electrical design or facility planning.

questions.prompt: Which statements match the deployment and operating model covered in Module 3?

choices.text: The 4-makes-3 rack power model uses four feeds, with three sufficient for full load.
The supplier model includes AVL, R-AVL, and RVL tiers to broaden availability.
Filters, pumps, the expansion tank, and the control panel are designed around field serviceability.
The specification is complete, closed, and not intended for community feedback.

questions.feedback_correct: Correct. Module 3 ties together power redundancy, supplier tiers, serviceability, and ongoing community involvement.
questions.feedback_incorrect: Review the final module takeaways. Deschutes is open and evolving, with deployment, service, and supply-chain patterns designed for scale.

slides.thank_you_message: Thank you for completing Module 3: Deployment, Supply Chain & Call to Action.
slides.cert_message: You've completed every module of the OCP Academy Project Deschutes course. Your next step: open the spec, evaluate it against your own deployment, and engage with the OCP CDU working group. Open is the key to scale.

term_glossary.tooltip: Direct liquid cooling removes heat at the chip or server level using cold plates and a dedicated coolant loop.
Coolant Distribution Unit: the liquid-to-liquid bridge that controls coolant flow, pressure, and heat transfer between facility water and IT equipment.
Facility Water System: the building-side chilled or condenser water loop feeding the CDU primary side.
Technology Cooling System: the clean secondary coolant loop serving rack manifolds and cold plates.
Difference between cold TCS supply leaving the CDU and cold FWS supply entering it; Deschutes targets 3 °C.
Differential pressure: the pressure head available to push coolant through manifolds, hoses, and cold plates.
Heat exchanger: transfers heat between loops without mixing the facility and IT coolant fluids.
Deionized water: water treated to remove dissolved ions that can affect corrosion or conductivity.
Propylene glycol coolant mixture at roughly 25% glycol concentration.
Ethylene propylene diene monomer: elastomer used for coolant-compatible hoses and seals.
Variable-frequency drive: controls motor speed, used here for pump speed control and efficiency.
Programmable logic controller: the industrial controller running CDU control loops, alarms, and interfaces.
Industrial communications protocol that carries Modbus messages over standard TCP/IP networking.
Network support for IPv4 and IPv6 at the same time.
Hot Aisle Containment: a structure that manages hot exhaust air around the rack row.
Rear-door heat exchanger: a rack rear-door cooling device referenced in the Redmond aisle dimensions.
Rack power arrangement where four feeds are present and any three can support full load.
Supplier tiers: active approved vendors, reserved approved vendors, and recommended or self-qualified vendors.
Field-replaceable unit: a component designed to be swapped during field service.
